tables.name: ErrorCategory
tables.description: Classification reference table for job record-level error patterns, used by Monitor-JobFlow.ps1 to determine whether total job failures should trigger alerts or be suppressed as expected business behavior.

columns.name: category_id
columns.description: PK

columns.name: error_type
columns.description: Pattern identifier extracted from error message. For action-based errors, this is the action name (e.g., CallRuleSet, SendNotice). For non-action errors, this is a pattern key (e.g., StaleData, UserInitiated)

columns.name: classification
columns.description: Category classification: TRUE_FAILURE, BUSINESS_REJECTION, or UNCLASSIFIED

columns.name: alert_on_total_failure
columns.description: Whether to generate a Jira ticket when this error type causes a total job failure. 1 = alert, 0 = suppress

columns.name: min_failure_threshold
columns.description: Minimum number of failed records before alerting. Prevents noise from low-volume sporadic failures

columns.name: description
columns.description: Human-readable description of the error type. Used in Jira ticket content to provide immediate context

columns.name: created_dttm
columns.description: When this category was created

columns.name: modified_dttm
columns.description: When this category was last modified

designNotes.summary: Most DM job failures are expected business behavior
designNotes.note: DM marks a job as Failed when any record is rejected. Most rejections are expected business behavior — consumers that do not meet mailing criteria, stale data from concurrent access, etc. ErrorCategory classifies each error pattern so validation can distinguish TRUE_FAILURE (alert) from BUSINESS_REJECTION (log only). Only 2 of 16 error types generate alerts; 13 are suppressed as business rejections.

designNotes.summary: How error_type values are derived from DM error messages
designNotes.note: DM error messages follow two patterns: action-based ("Failed to execute action: ActionName (ENTITY)") where the action name is extracted, and non-action patterns matched directly (e.g., "Data is stale" ? StaleData, "User with Id:" ? UserInitiated). The error_type column stores the extracted pattern for lookup.

statusValues.meaning: Genuine system or configuration error requiring investigation. Generates Jira ticket on total failure.
Records rejected due to expected business rule conditions. System is working correctly. No ticket generated.
Unknown error pattern. Alerts to ensure new patterns are investigated and classified.

queries.name: Alertable Error Types
queries.description: Shows error types that will generate a Jira ticket on total job failure.

queries.name: Simulate Alert Decision
queries.description: Tests the alert-vs-suppress logic for a given error type and failure count, matching what Monitor-JobFlow uses in Step-ValidateCompletedFlows.
queries.sql:
DECLARE @error_type VARCHAR(100) = 'CallRuleSet';
DECLARE @failed_count INT = 15;

SELECT 
    error_type,
    classification,
    alert_on_total_failure,
    CASE 
        WHEN alert_on_total_failure = 1 
         AND @failed_count >= min_failure_threshold 
        THEN 'ALERT'
        ELSE 'SUPPRESS'
    END AS action
FROM JobFlow.ErrorCategory
WHERE error_type = @error_type;

tables.name: FlowConfig
tables.description: Per-flow monitoring configuration including active status, scheduling expectations, alert settings, and synchronization with Debt Manager.
tables.dataFlow: Monitor-JobFlow.ps1 Step-ConfigSync: INSERT new flows (is_monitored=0, expected_schedule=UNCONFIGURED) | UPDATE dm_is_active and dm_last_sync_dttm for existing flows | Queues Jira ticket for new/deactivated flows.

columns.name: config_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for this configuration row

columns.name: job_sqnc_id
columns.description: Flow ID from crs5_oltp.dbo.job_sqnc. Unique constraint

columns.name: job_sqnc_shrt_nm
columns.description: Flow short name (e.g., JFDNEV). Denormalized for convenience

columns.name: job_sqnc_nm
columns.description: Full flow name

columns.name: dm_is_active
columns.description: Whether flow is active in DM (job_sqnc_actv_flg = 'Y'). NULL if never synced

columns.name: dm_last_sync_dttm
columns.description: When ConfigSync last verified this flow against DM

columns.name: is_monitored
columns.description: Whether xFACts should monitor this flow. Set to 0 to exclude from processing

columns.name: expected_schedule
columns.description: Human-readable schedule description (e.g., 'DAILY', 'WEEKDAYS', 'WEEKLY')

columns.name: expected_max_duration_hours
columns.description: Expected maximum runtime for duration alerting. Reserved for future use

columns.name: alert_on_missing
columns.description: Generate alerts if flow doesn't start within expected window

columns.name: alert_on_critical_failure
columns.description: Generate alerts if critical jobs fail within this flow

columns.name: effective_start_date
columns.description: When this configuration becomes active

columns.name: effective_end_date
columns.description: When this configuration expires. NULL = no expiration

columns.name: created_dttm
columns.description: When this row was created

columns.name: created_by
columns.description: Who created this row

columns.name: modified_dttm
columns.description: Last modification timestamp

columns.name: modified_by
columns.description: Who last modified this row

columns.name: notes
columns.description: Free-form notes about this flow configuration

checkConstraints.name: CK_FlowConfig_ExpectedSchedule
checkConstraints.definition: ([expected_schedule]='UNCONFIGURED' OR [expected_schedule]='ON-DEMAND' OR [expected_schedule]='VARIABLE' OR [expected_schedule]='EVERY_N_HOURS' OR [expected_schedule]='MONTHLY' OR [expected_schedule]='WEEKLY' OR [expected_schedule]='DAILY')

designNotes.summary: How FlowConfig stays synchronized with Debt Manager
designNotes.note: FlowConfig is automatically maintained by Monitor-JobFlow.ps1 Step-ConfigSync, which runs as the first step of every monitoring cycle. New flows detected in DM are inserted with is_monitored = 0 and expected_schedule = UNCONFIGURED, triggering a Jira ticket for configuration. Deactivated flows get dm_is_active set to 0 with a Jira ticket. Reactivated flows get dm_is_active reset to 1 silently. The dm_last_sync_dttm field tracks when each flow was last verified against DM.

designNotes.summary: Future-dated configuration and historical tracking
designNotes.note: effective_start_date and effective_end_date support future-dated configuration changes. A configuration with an end date can be superseded by a new row with a later start date. NULL effective_end_date means no expiration. Check constraints enforce end_date >= start_date when end_date is populated.

statusValues.meaning: Flow runs every day.
Flow runs Monday through Friday.
Flow runs on a specific day of the week.
Flow runs on a specific day of the month.
Flow runs multiple times per day at a fixed interval.
Flow has a non-standard schedule.
Flow runs only when manually triggered.
New flow detected by ConfigSync — awaiting schedule configuration. Not included in missing flow detection.

queries.name: Active Monitored Flows
queries.description: Lists all flows that are both monitored by xFACts and active in Debt Manager, with their alert settings.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    job_sqnc_shrt_nm AS flow_code,
    job_sqnc_nm AS flow_name,
    expected_schedule,
    alert_on_missing,
    alert_on_critical_failure
FROM JobFlow.FlowConfig
WHERE is_monitored = 1
  AND dm_is_active = 1
ORDER BY job_sqnc_shrt_nm;

queries.name: Sync Status Check
queries.description: Shows when each flow was last verified against Debt Manager. Large hours_since_sync values indicate ConfigSync may not be running.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    job_sqnc_shrt_nm,
    dm_is_active,
    dm_last_sync_dttm,
    DATEDIFF(HOUR, dm_last_sync_dttm, GETDATE()) AS hours_since_sync
FROM JobFlow.FlowConfig
ORDER BY dm_last_sync_dttm DESC;

queries.name: Flows Not In DM
queries.description: Finds flows that are deactivated or have never been synced with Debt Manager — useful for drift detection.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    job_sqnc_shrt_nm,
    dm_is_active,
    notes
FROM JobFlow.FlowConfig
WHERE dm_is_active = 0 OR dm_is_active IS NULL
ORDER BY job_sqnc_shrt_nm;

relationshipNotes.note: FlowExecutionTracking.job_sqnc_id references FlowConfig.job_sqnc_id (UQ). Each flow config can have many execution tracking records over time — one per execution instance.
Schedule.job_sqnc_id references FlowConfig.job_sqnc_id. A flow can have multiple schedule rows (e.g., weekday schedule + weekend schedule).

The job_sqnc_id and job_sqnc_shrt_nm columns originate from crs5_oltp.dbo.job_sqnc. ConfigSync queries DM for active flows (job_sqnc_actv_flg = Y) and synchronizes dm_is_active and dm_last_sync_dttm each cycle.

tables.name: FlowExecutionTracking
tables.description: The core state machine table that tracks each flow execution instance from detection through completion.
tables.dataFlow: Step-DetectFlows: INSERT new rows (state=DETECTED). Step-UpdateFlowProgress: UPDATE job counts. Step-TransitionFlowStates: UPDATE execution_state. Step-ValidateCompletedFlows: UPDATE is_validated and validation_dttm. Step-DetectStalls: UPDATE execution_state to STALLED or back to EXECUTING.

columns.name: tracking_id
columns.description: PK

columns.name: job_sqnc_log_id
columns.description: FK

columns.name: job_sqnc_id
columns.description: The flow definition ID from crs5_oltp.dbo.job_sqnc. Does not change between executions

columns.name: job_sqnc_shrt_nm
columns.description: Flow short name (e.g., JFDNEV, JFD4HR). Denormalized for query convenience

columns.name: execution_date
columns.description: Calendar date of the execution. Used with execution_sequence for daily uniqueness

columns.name: execution_sequence
columns.description: Sequential run number for this flow on this date (1st run = 1, 2nd run = 2, etc.)

columns.name: execution_state
columns.description: Current state: DETECTED, EXECUTING, COMPLETE, FAILED, CANCELLED

columns.name: first_detected_dttm
columns.description: When sp_StateMonitor first detected this execution

columns.name: last_activity_dttm
columns.description: Last time any change was recorded for this execution

columns.name: completion_dttm
columns.description: When execution_state transitioned to COMPLETE

columns.name: validation_dttm
columns.description: When post-completion validation was run (if applicable)

columns.name: is_validated
columns.description: Set to 1 after Step-ValidateCompletedFlows processes the flow. Prevents duplicate validation

columns.name: execution_window_start
columns.description: When the flow execution began in the source system (from job_sqnc_exctn_dttm)

columns.name: execution_window_end
columns.description: When the last job in the flow completed

columns.name: expected_job_count
columns.description: Number of jobs defined in the flow at detection time

columns.name: completed_job_count
columns.description: Jobs with terminal success status

columns.name: executing_job_count
columns.description: Jobs currently running (job_stts_cd = 1)

columns.name: pending_job_count
columns.description: Jobs waiting to start (job_stts_cd = 6)

columns.name: failed_job_count
columns.description: Jobs that failed (job_stts_cd = 2 with zero records processed)

columns.name: cancelled_job_count
columns.description: Jobs that were cancelled

columns.name: expected_jobs_json
columns.description: JSON array of expected jobs captured at detection time. Preserves flow definition even if it changes later

columns.name: aggregate_completed_records
columns.description: Total records processed across all completed jobs in this execution

columns.name: last_poll_completed_records
columns.description: Records completed as of the previous poll. Used to calculate throughput

columns.name: created_dttm
columns.description: When this tracking record was created

columns.name: modified_dttm
columns.description: Last modification timestamp. Updated on every poll that touches this row

checkConstraints.name: CK_FlowExecutionTracking_State
checkConstraints.definition: ([execution_state]='HISTORICAL' OR [execution_state]='ABANDONED' OR [execution_state]='STALLED' OR [execution_state]='VALIDATED' OR [execution_state]='COMPLETE' OR [execution_state]='EXECUTING' OR [execution_state]='DETECTED')

designNotes.summary: Why execution instances use the source system log ID
designNotes.note: Each flow can execute multiple times per day. The job_sqnc_log_id from crs5_oltp.dbo.job_sqnc_log uniquely identifies each execution instance, preventing incorrect aggregation of multiple daily runs. Added in v1.1.0 after discovering that tracking by job_sqnc_id alone merged separate executions.

designNotes.summary: Flow state transitions from detection through validation
designNotes.note: States: DETECTED ? EXECUTING ? COMPLETE ? VALIDATED (happy path). EXECUTING can also transition to STALLED (no progress for 30+ minutes), which returns to EXECUTING when progress resumes. ABANDONED is a manual terminal state. State transitions are managed by Monitor-JobFlow.ps1 Step-TransitionFlowStates.

designNotes.summary: Historical data distinguished by tracking_id = 0
designNotes.note: Records with tracking_id = 0 were backfilled from dm_history and represent executions that occurred before real-time monitoring began. These rows have NULL job_sqnc_log_id and may have incomplete job counts.

statusValues.meaning: Flow execution found in source system. Jobs not yet started.
At least one job has begun processing.
All expected jobs have finished. Awaiting validation.
Post-execution validation has run. Terminal state for happy path.
No system-wide progress detected for 30+ minutes. Returns to EXECUTING on recovery.
Flow manually abandoned. Terminal state.

queries.name: Currently Executing Flows
queries.description: Shows all flow executions currently in progress with job counts and start time.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    tracking_id,
    job_sqnc_shrt_nm AS flow_code,
    execution_state,
    executing_job_count,
    pending_job_count,
    completed_job_count,
    execution_window_start
FROM JobFlow.FlowExecutionTracking
WHERE execution_state IN ('DETECTED', 'EXECUTING')
ORDER BY execution_window_start;

queries.name: Today's Flow Summary
queries.description: Daily overview of all flow executions with state, job counts, record totals, and duration.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    job_sqnc_shrt_nm AS flow_code,
    execution_sequence,
    execution_state,
    expected_job_count,
    completed_job_count,
    failed_job_count,
    aggregate_completed_records,
    DATEDIFF(MINUTE, execution_window_start, ISNULL(completion_dttm, GETDATE())) AS duration_minutes
FROM JobFlow.FlowExecutionTracking
WHERE execution_date = CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE)
ORDER BY execution_window_start;

queries.name: Flows With Multiple Daily Runs
queries.description: Identifies flows that executed more than once on the same day over the past 30 days — useful for understanding scheduling patterns and detecting anomalies.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    job_sqnc_shrt_nm,
    execution_date,
    COUNT(*) AS run_count
FROM JobFlow.FlowExecutionTracking
WHERE execution_date >= DATEADD(DAY, -30, GETDATE())
GROUP BY job_sqnc_shrt_nm, execution_date
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY execution_date DESC, run_count DESC;

queries.name: Flows Pending Validation
queries.description: Finds completed flow executions that have not yet been validated — indicates Step-ValidateCompletedFlows may need attention.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    tracking_id,
    job_sqnc_shrt_nm,
    execution_date,
    completion_dttm
FROM JobFlow.FlowExecutionTracking
WHERE execution_state = 'COMPLETE'
  AND validation_dttm IS NULL
  AND completion_dttm IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY completion_dttm;

relationshipNotes.note: FlowExecutionTracking.job_sqnc_id references FlowConfig.job_sqnc_id. Many execution rows per flow config — one per execution instance.
JobExecutionLog.tracking_id references FlowExecutionTracking.tracking_id. One execution can have many job completion records.
ValidationLog.tracking_id references FlowExecutionTracking.tracking_id. One validation per completed execution.
The job_sqnc_log_id column links to crs5_oltp.dbo.job_sqnc_log. Step-DetectFlows queries this table for new execution instances not yet tracked in xFACts.

tables.name: JobConfig
tables.description: Job-level configuration including DM synchronization, criticality classification, and fixed date logic detection for prioritized alerting.

columns.name: config_id
columns.description: PK

columns.name: job_id
columns.description: UQ

columns.name: job_shrt_nm
columns.description: Job short name (e.g., NBIMPORT). Denormalized

columns.name: job_nm
columns.description: Full job name

columns.name: dm_is_active
columns.description: Whether job is active in DM. NULL if never synced

columns.name: dm_last_sync_dttm
columns.description: When last verified against DM

columns.name: last_execution_dttm
columns.description: When job last executed (from DM history)

columns.name: criticality_reason
columns.description: Explanation of why job is marked critical

columns.name: effective_start_date
columns.description: When this configuration becomes active

columns.name: effective_end_date
columns.description: When this configuration expires. NULL = no expiration

columns.name: created_dttm
columns.description: When this row was created

columns.name: created_by
columns.description: Who created this row

columns.name: modified_dttm
columns.description: Last modification timestamp

columns.name: modified_by
columns.description: Who last modified this row

columns.name: notes
columns.description: Free-form notes about this job configuration

columns.name: has_fixed_date_logic
columns.description: Job filter uses fixed dates (e.g., WHERE date = TODAY). Records permanently lost if missed

columns.name: is_business_critical
columns.description: Job is essential for daily operations regardless of date logic

columns.name: is_critical
columns.description: 1 if either flag is set, 0 otherwise. Computed persisted column

designNotes.summary: Two independent reasons a job can be critical
designNotes.note: has_fixed_date_logic flags jobs whose SQL filters use today-only date logic — records are permanently missed if the job does not run on schedule. is_business_critical flags jobs essential to daily operations regardless of date logic. The computed is_critical column (persisted) is 1 if either flag is set, simplifying queries while preserving the reason.

designNotes.summary: Initial load from DM history, ongoing via ConfigSync
designNotes.note: Initial population from 313 historical jobs found in DM execution history. Criticality flags populated through team review of job filter logic and business requirements. ConfigSync maintains dm_is_active and dm_last_sync_dttm for drift detection.

queries.name: Critical Jobs Summary
queries.description: Lists all jobs marked critical (either fixed date logic or business critical) with their classification reason.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    job_shrt_nm,
    job_nm,
    has_fixed_date_logic,
    is_business_critical,
    criticality_reason
FROM JobFlow.JobConfig
WHERE is_critical = 1
ORDER BY job_shrt_nm;

queries.name: Jobs by Criticality Type
queries.description: Distribution of jobs across criticality categories — helps assess alert coverage.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN has_fixed_date_logic = 1 AND is_business_critical = 1 THEN 'Both'
        WHEN has_fixed_date_logic = 1 THEN 'Fixed Date Logic'
        WHEN is_business_critical = 1 THEN 'Business Critical'
        ELSE 'Not Critical'
    END AS criticality_type,
    COUNT(*) AS job_count
FROM JobFlow.JobConfig
GROUP BY 
    CASE 
        WHEN has_fixed_date_logic = 1 AND is_business_critical = 1 THEN 'Both'
        WHEN has_fixed_date_logic = 1 THEN 'Fixed Date Logic'
        WHEN is_business_critical = 1 THEN 'Business Critical'
        ELSE 'Not Critical'
    END
ORDER BY job_count DESC;

queries.name: Jobs Not Recently Executed
queries.description: Finds active jobs that haven't executed in 30+ days — may indicate deactivated or orphaned job definitions.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    job_shrt_nm,
    last_execution_dttm,
    DATEDIFF(DAY, last_execution_dttm, GETDATE()) AS days_since_execution
FROM JobFlow.JobConfig
WHERE dm_is_active = 1
  AND (last_execution_dttm IS NULL OR last_execution_dttm < DATEADD(DAY, -30, GETDATE()))
ORDER BY last_execution_dttm;

relationshipNotes.note: Step-ValidateCompletedFlows joins JobConfig to determine if a missing or failed job is critical. The is_critical flag and criticality_reason drive alert severity and Jira ticket content.
The job_id and job_shrt_nm columns originate from crs5_oltp.dbo.job. ConfigSync synchronizes dm_is_active status from DM.

tables.name: JobExecutionLog
tables.description: Captures detailed execution history for individual jobs within flow executions, preserving metrics at the moment of completion.
tables.dataFlow: Queries crs5_oltp.dbo.job_log for jobs with terminal status codes (2, 3, 4) not already in JobExecutionLog (dedup via NOT EXISTS on job_log_id). Calculates job_status using status translation rules, captures record counts, timing metrics, and error messages.

columns.name: execution_detail_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each job execution record

columns.name: tracking_id
columns.description: Links to FlowExecutionTracking. NULL or 0 for backfilled/orphaned records

columns.name: job_log_id
columns.description: Links to crs5_oltp.dbo.job_log. The source system execution ID

columns.name: job_id
columns.description: Job definition ID from crs5_oltp.dbo.job

columns.name: job_shrt_nm
columns.description: Job short name (e.g., NBIMPORT, INTCALC). Denormalized for convenience

columns.name: job_nm
columns.description: Full job name. May be NULL for older records

columns.name: job_sqnc_id
columns.description: Flow ID this job executed within. NULL if job ran outside a flow

columns.name: job_sqnc_shrt_nm
columns.description: Flow short name. NULL if job ran outside a flow

columns.name: execution_order_nmbr
columns.description: Order position within the flow (1st job, 2nd job, etc.)

columns.name: execution_type
columns.description: How the job was triggered: SCHEDULED or AD_HOC

columns.name: executed_by
columns.description: Username that initiated the execution

columns.name: execution_date
columns.description: Calendar date of execution. Used for daily aggregations

columns.name: job_exec_dttm
columns.description: When the job execution was initiated (from job_log)

columns.name: job_start_dttm
columns.description: When the first record began processing

columns.name: job_end_dttm
columns.description: When the last record finished processing

columns.name: job_reported_complete_dttm
columns.description: When DM marked the job complete (status change timestamp)

columns.name: total_records
columns.description: Total records assigned to this job (job_entty_ttl_nmbr). NULL if job had no records

columns.name: succeeded_count
columns.description: Records that processed without errors

columns.name: failed_count
columns.description: Records that failed business rule validation

columns.name: records_per_second
columns.description: Calculated throughput rate

columns.name: execution_time_seconds
columns.description: Total execution duration in seconds

columns.name: job_status
columns.description: xFACts status: EXECUTING, PENDING, CANCELLED, CANCELLING, FAILED, COMPLETED, PARTIAL, UNKNOWN

columns.name: job_stts_cd
columns.description: Raw DM status code (1=Started, 2=Failed, 3=Complete, etc.)

columns.name: error_message
columns.description: Error text from DM if job failed. Sanitized of PII

columns.name: captured_dttm
columns.description: When xFACts captured this completion record

indexes.name: IX_JobExecutionLog_ByDate
indexes.includedColumns: job_sqnc_shrt_nm, job_shrt_nm, job_start_dttm, job_end_dttm, total_records, succeeded_count, failed_count, job_status

checkConstraints.name: CK_JobExecutionLog_Status
checkConstraints.definition: ([job_status]='UNKNOWN' OR [job_status]='PARTIAL' OR [job_status]='COMPLETED' OR [job_status]='FAILED' OR [job_status]='CANCELLING' OR [job_status]='CANCELLED' OR [job_status]='PENDING' OR [job_status]='EXECUTING')

designNotes.summary: xFACts job status differs from raw DM status codes
designNotes.note: DM marks a job as Failed (status 2) if any record fails validation. xFACts uses PARTIAL when records were successfully processed despite some rejections, reserving FAILED for true technical failures where no work was done. This prevents false alarms on jobs that are working correctly but have expected business rule rejections.

designNotes.summary: captured_dttm vs actual completion time
designNotes.note: Rows are inserted when Monitor-JobFlow.ps1 Step-CaptureCompletedJobs detects a terminal status. captured_dttm is when xFACts recorded the event, not when the job actually finished. The job_reported_complete_dttm from DM is the actual completion time. The gap depends on polling interval (up to ~5 minutes).

designNotes.summary: Continuous growth table
designNotes.note: Approximately 200-400 new rows per night during normal operations. Historical data spans back to June 2020 via backfill from dm_history. Records with tracking_id = 0 indicate pre-monitoring backfill.

statusValues.meaning: Job completed successfully. All records processed or DM status 3 with no failures.
Job processed some records but some failed business validation. DM status 2 or 3 with both succeeded and failed counts.
True technical failure — DM status 2 with zero records successfully processed.
Job cancelled by user. DM status 4.
Job still running. DM status 1. Should not appear in completed job log under normal operation.
Job queued, waiting to start. DM status 6.
Unmapped DM status code. Indicates a new status not yet added to translation logic.

queries.name: Jobs Completed Today
queries.description: All job completions for today with status, record counts, and duration.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    job_shrt_nm,
    job_sqnc_shrt_nm AS flow_code,
    job_status,
    total_records,
    succeeded_count,
    failed_count,
    execution_time_seconds
FROM JobFlow.JobExecutionLog
WHERE execution_date = CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE)
ORDER BY captured_dttm DESC;

queries.name: Failed Jobs in Date Range
queries.description: Shows jobs with FAILED or PARTIAL status over the past 7 days with error messages for troubleshooting.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    execution_date,
    job_shrt_nm,
    job_sqnc_shrt_nm AS flow_code,
    job_status,
    error_message
FROM JobFlow.JobExecutionLog
WHERE job_status IN ('FAILED', 'PARTIAL')
  AND execution_date >= DATEADD(DAY, -7, GETDATE())
ORDER BY execution_date DESC, captured_dttm DESC;

queries.name: Job Execution Trends
queries.description: Aggregated performance metrics per job over the past 30 days — execution count, average duration, throughput, and total records processed.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    job_shrt_nm,
    COUNT(*) AS execution_count,
    AVG(execution_time_seconds) AS avg_duration_sec,
    AVG(records_per_second) AS avg_throughput,
    SUM(succeeded_count) AS total_records_processed
FROM JobFlow.JobExecutionLog
WHERE execution_date >= DATEADD(DAY, -30, GETDATE())
GROUP BY job_shrt_nm
ORDER BY execution_count DESC;

relationshipNotes.note: JobExecutionLog.tracking_id references FlowExecutionTracking.tracking_id. NULL or 0 tracking_id indicates backfilled or orphaned records not captured by real-time monitoring.
The job_log_id column links to crs5_oltp.dbo.job_log. Record counts come from job_entty_log aggregation. IX_JobExecutionLog_JobLogId supports NOT EXISTS deduplication during capture.

tables.name: JobStatus
tables.description: Reference table mapping Debt Manager job status codes to human-readable descriptions and xFACts-effective status interpretations.

columns.name: job_stts_cd
columns.description: Primary key. DM status code (1-6)

columns.name: job_stts_val_txt
columns.description: Short status name from DM (e.g., 'Started', 'Failed')

columns.name: job_stts_dscrptn_txt
columns.description: Full description of what this status means

columns.name: xfacts_effective_status
columns.description: xFACts interpretation: COMPLETED, PARTIAL, FAILED, CANCELLED, EXECUTING, PENDING

columns.name: is_terminal
columns.description: 1 if this is a final state, 0 if job is still in progress

designNotes.summary: Maps DM numeric codes to human-readable status descriptions
designNotes.note: Static reference table with 6 rows — one per DM job_stts_cd value (1-6). The xfacts_effective_status column provides the xFACts interpretation which can differ from DM. For example, DM status 2 (Failed) maps to either PARTIAL or FAILED in xFACts depending on whether any records processed successfully. The is_terminal flag indicates final vs in-progress states.

tables.name: Schedule
tables.description: Expected execution schedules for job flows, used by missing flow detection to identify when flows should have started.

columns.name: schedule_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for this schedule row

columns.name: job_sqnc_id
columns.description: Flow ID from Config table

columns.name: job_sqnc_shrt_nm
columns.description: Flow short name. Denormalized for convenience

columns.name: schedule_type
columns.description: Type: DAILY, WEEKDAYS, WEEKLY, MONTHLY, EVERY_N_HOURS

columns.name: schedule_frequency
columns.description: For EVERY_N_HOURS: interval in hours

columns.name: schedule_day_of_week
columns.description: For WEEKLY: 1=Sunday through 7=Saturday

columns.name: schedule_day_of_month
columns.description: For MONTHLY: day of month (1-31)

columns.name: schedule_week_of_month
columns.description: For MONTHLY: week of month (1-5)

columns.name: expected_start_time
columns.description: Time of day the flow should start

columns.name: start_time_tolerance_minutes
columns.description: Minutes after expected time before alerting

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Whether this schedule is currently active

columns.name: alert_on_missing
columns.description: Whether to alert if flow misses this schedule

columns.name: effective_start_date
columns.description: When this schedule becomes active

columns.name: effective_end_date
columns.description: When this schedule expires. NULL = no expiration

columns.name: created_dttm
columns.description: When this row was created

columns.name: created_by
columns.description: Who created this row

columns.name: modified_dttm
columns.description: Last modification timestamp

columns.name: modified_by
columns.description: Who last modified this row

columns.name: notes
columns.description: Free-form notes about this schedule

designNotes.summary: A flow can have different schedules for different days
designNotes.note: A flow can have multiple schedule rows. For example, a flow might run at 10 PM on weekdays and 8 AM on weekends. Each schedule row has its own tolerance window, active flag, and alert setting. The Schedule table supports DAILY, WEEKDAYS, WEEKLY, MONTHLY, and EVERY_N_HOURS schedule types.

designNotes.summary: Buffer time before missing flow alert fires
designNotes.note: start_time_tolerance_minutes provides buffer after the expected start time before a missing flow alert fires. Default is 60 minutes. Critical nightly flows might use 60 min, hourly flows 15 min. The detection logic evaluates: current time > expected_start_time + tolerance AND no execution exists for today.

statusValues.meaning: Runs every day at expected_start_time.
Runs Monday through Friday at expected_start_time.
Runs on schedule_day_of_week at expected_start_time.
Runs on schedule_day_of_month at expected_start_time.
Runs every schedule_frequency hours starting at expected_start_time.
Non-standard schedule requiring manual evaluation.

queries.name: Active Schedules with Deadlines
queries.description: All active schedules configured for missing flow alerts, with calculated deadline times.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    job_sqnc_shrt_nm AS flow_code,
    schedule_type,
    expected_start_time,
    start_time_tolerance_minutes,
    DATEADD(MINUTE, start_time_tolerance_minutes, 
            CAST(expected_start_time AS DATETIME)) AS deadline_time
FROM JobFlow.Schedule
WHERE is_active = 1
  AND alert_on_missing = 1
ORDER BY expected_start_time;

queries.name: Today's Expected Flows
queries.description: Evaluates schedule types and day-of-week rules to show which flows should execute today.
queries.sql:
DECLARE @today_dow INT = DATEPART(WEEKDAY, GETDATE());

SELECT 
    job_sqnc_shrt_nm AS flow_code,
    schedule_type,
    expected_start_time
FROM JobFlow.Schedule
WHERE is_active = 1
  AND (
      schedule_type = 'DAILY'
      OR (schedule_type = 'WEEKDAYS' AND @today_dow BETWEEN 2 AND 6)
      OR (schedule_type = 'WEEKLY' AND schedule_day_of_week = @today_dow)
      OR (schedule_type = 'MONTHLY' AND schedule_day_of_month = DAY(GETDATE()))
      OR schedule_type = 'EVERY_N_HOURS'
  )
ORDER BY expected_start_time;

queries.name: Flows Past Deadline
queries.description: Identifies flows that should have started by now but have no execution recorded today — the same logic Step-DetectMissingFlows uses.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    s.job_sqnc_shrt_nm,
    s.expected_start_time,
    s.start_time_tolerance_minutes,
    DATEADD(MINUTE, s.start_time_tolerance_minutes, 
            CAST(CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE) AS DATETIME) + CAST(s.expected_start_time AS DATETIME)) AS deadline
FROM JobFlow.Schedule s
WHERE s.is_active = 1
  AND GETDATE() > DATEADD(MINUTE, s.start_time_tolerance_minutes, 
            CAST(CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE) AS DATETIME) + CAST(s.expected_start_time AS DATETIME))
  AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM JobFlow.FlowExecutionTracking t
      WHERE t.job_sqnc_id = s.job_sqnc_id
        AND t.execution_date = CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE)
  );

relationshipNotes.note: Schedule.job_sqnc_id references FlowConfig.job_sqnc_id. Multiple schedule rows per flow are supported. Only active schedules (is_active = 1) with alert_on_missing = 1 trigger missing flow detection.

tables.name: StallDetectionLog
tables.description: Append-only audit log capturing stall detection events with diagnostic XML snapshots for troubleshooting and historical analysis.

columns.name: log_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each log entry

columns.name: task_id
columns.description: Links to Orchestrator_ExecutionDetail for the poll that generated this event

columns.name: poll_dttm
columns.description: When the poll occurred

columns.name: counter_before
columns.description: no_progress_poll_count value before this event

columns.name: counter_after
columns.description: no_progress_poll_count value after this event

columns.name: stall_threshold
columns.description: Configured threshold at time of event (typically 6)

columns.name: threshold_reached
columns.description: 1 if counter_after >= stall_threshold

columns.name: event_type
columns.description: Event classification: INCREMENT, ALERT, STALLED, RESET

columns.name: snapshot_comparison_xml
columns.description: Full snapshot data including job lists, cross-poll comparison, and progress indicators

columns.name: jira_queued
columns.description: 1 if a Jira ticket was queued for this event

columns.name: teams_queued
columns.description: 1 if a Teams alert was queued for this event

columns.name: created_dttm
columns.description: When this log entry was created

designNotes.summary: Complete history of stall detection events
designNotes.note: Rows are never updated or deleted. Events are only logged when the counter changes (INCREMENT, RESET, ALERT, STALLED), not on every poll. This keeps the table manageable while capturing all meaningful stall detection activity. Normal nights produce 0-2 rows. Actual stall events produce 7+ rows.

designNotes.summary: Authoritative source for stall alert deduplication
designNotes.note: Stall alerts are suppressed if an ALERT event exists today with no subsequent RESET. This allows multiple alerts per day when a stall-recovery-stall cycle occurs — a second stall after recovery generates a fresh ticket. Replaces the previous stall_alert_sent_dttm approach.

statusValues.meaning: No progress detected. Counter increased by 1. Building toward threshold.
Counter reached threshold (default: 6). Stall confirmed. Jira ticket and Teams alert queued.
Continuing stall after ALERT. Counter still incrementing. No additional alerts.
Progress detected. Counter reset to 0. Stall episode ended.

queries.name: Today's Stall Events
queries.description: All stall detection events for today — shows the counter progression and whether alerts were fired.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    poll_dttm,
    event_type,
    counter_before,
    counter_after,
    jira_queued,
    teams_queued
FROM JobFlow.StallDetectionLog
WHERE CAST(poll_dttm AS DATE) = CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE)
ORDER BY poll_dttm;

queries.name: Recent ALERT Events
queries.description: Shows actual stall alerts fired in the past 7 days with diagnostic snapshot XML for investigation.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    poll_dttm,
    counter_before,
    counter_after,
    jira_queued,
    teams_queued,
    snapshot_comparison_xml
FROM JobFlow.StallDetectionLog
WHERE event_type = 'ALERT'
  AND poll_dttm >= DATEADD(DAY, -7, GETDATE())
ORDER BY poll_dttm DESC;

queries.name: Check Active Stall Status
queries.description: Determines whether there is an active stall right now (ALERT fired today with no subsequent RESET) — matches the deduplication logic in Monitor-JobFlow.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN EXISTS (
            SELECT 1 FROM JobFlow.StallDetectionLog sdl
            WHERE CAST(sdl.poll_dttm AS DATE) = CAST(GETDATE() AS DATE)
              AND sdl.event_type = 'ALERT'
              AND NOT EXISTS (
                  SELECT 1 FROM JobFlow.StallDetectionLog r
                  WHERE r.poll_dttm > sdl.poll_dttm
                    AND r.event_type = 'RESET'
              )
        )
        THEN 'Active stall - alerts suppressed'
        ELSE 'No active stall - new alert would fire'
    END AS stall_status;

queries.name: Extract Jobs from XML Snapshot
queries.description: Shreds the diagnostic XML to show individual jobs that were executing/pending during a stall event. Replace @log_id with the target log entry.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    log_id,
    poll_dttm,
    event_type,
    j.value('@id', 'INT') AS job_log_id,
    j.value('@status', 'INT') AS job_status,
    j.value('@count', 'INT') AS processed_count
FROM JobFlow.StallDetectionLog
CROSS APPLY snapshot_comparison_xml.nodes('//Snapshot1/Job') AS t(j)
WHERE log_id = @log_id;

relationshipNotes.note: The detail_id column links to the Orchestrator execution detail for the monitoring cycle that detected the stall event. Enables correlation with orchestrator timing and other module activity during the same cycle.

tables.name: Status
tables.description: Tracks execution state and health metrics for each step of the Monitor-JobFlow.ps1 script, including stall detection snapshots.
tables.dataFlow: At step start: UPDATE started_dttm. At step end: UPDATE completed_dttm, last_status (SUCCESS/FAILED), last_result_count, last_error_message. DetectStalls additionally updates stall_snapshot_xml and stall_no_progress_count.

columns.name: status_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each status row

columns.name: process_name
columns.description: Step name: ConfigSync, DetectFlows, CaptureJobs, UpdateProgress, TransitionStates, DetectStalls, ValidateFlows, DetectMissing

columns.name: started_dttm
columns.description: When the step began execution

columns.name: completed_dttm
columns.description: When the step finished execution

columns.name: last_status
columns.description: Result of last execution: SUCCESS or FAILED

columns.name: last_result_count
columns.description: Number of items processed (flows detected, jobs captured, etc.)

columns.name: last_error_message
columns.description: Error details if last_status = FAILED

columns.name: stall_no_progress_count
columns.description: Counter of consecutive polls with no progress. Resets to 0 when progress detected

columns.name: stall_last_progress_dttm
columns.description: When meaningful progress was last detected during stall evaluation. Updated each poll cycle when the executing job snapshot differs from the previous cycle. Used with stall_no_progress_count to determine whether a flow has stalled.

columns.name: stall_snapshot_xml
columns.description: XML snapshot of current executing/pending job_log_ids for comparison

columns.name: created_dttm
columns.description: When the row was created

designNotes.summary: Exactly 8 rows — one per processing step
designNotes.note: The table contains exactly 8 rows corresponding to the processing steps in Monitor-JobFlow.ps1: ConfigSync (0), DetectFlows (1), CaptureJobs (2), UpdateProgress (3), TransitionStates (4), DetectStalls (5), ValidateFlows (6), DetectMissing (7). Rows are never inserted or deleted during normal operation — only updated.

designNotes.summary: DetectStalls row carries inter-cycle state
designNotes.note: The DetectStalls row stores stall_snapshot_xml (XML snapshot of executing/pending job_log_ids) and stall_no_progress_count (consecutive polls with no change). This state persists between Monitor-JobFlow.ps1 invocations, enabling cross-poll comparison without additional tables.

queries.name: Current Step Status
queries.description: Dashboard view of all eight processing steps — last status, result count, and minutes since last run.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    process_name,
    last_status,
    last_result_count,
    completed_dttm,
    DATEDIFF(MINUTE, completed_dttm, GETDATE()) AS minutes_ago
FROM JobFlow.Status
ORDER BY status_id;

queries.name: Recent Step Failures
queries.description: Shows any processing steps that failed in the past 24 hours with error details.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    process_name,
    last_status,
    last_error_message,
    completed_dttm
FROM JobFlow.Status
WHERE last_status = 'FAILED'
  AND completed_dttm >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, GETDATE());

queries.name: Stall Detection State
queries.description: Quick check of the current stall counter and snapshot without digging into StallDetectionLog.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    stall_no_progress_count,
    stall_snapshot_xml,
    completed_dttm AS last_check
FROM JobFlow.Status
WHERE process_name = 'DetectStalls';

queries.name: Verify Script Is Running
queries.description: Health check — if DetectFlows completed more than 10 minutes ago, Monitor-JobFlow may not be running.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    process_name,
    completed_dttm,
    DATEDIFF(MINUTE, completed_dttm, GETDATE()) AS minutes_since_last_run
FROM JobFlow.Status
WHERE process_name = 'DetectFlows'
  AND DATEDIFF(MINUTE, completed_dttm, GETDATE()) > 10;

tables.name: ValidationException
tables.description: Detailed exception records for individual job-level issues found during flow execution validation, including missing jobs, failed jobs, and order discrepancies.

columns.name: exception_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for this exception record

columns.name: validation_id
columns.description: Links to parent ValidationLog record

columns.name: job_id
columns.description: Job definition ID from crs5_oltp.dbo.job

columns.name: job_shrt_nm
columns.description: Job short name

columns.name: job_nm
columns.description: Full job name

columns.name: exception_type
columns.description: Type: MISSING, FAILED, UNEXPECTED, ORDER_MISMATCH, DUPLICATE

columns.name: is_critical
columns.description: 1 if this job is marked critical in JobConfig

columns.name: expected_order_nmbr
columns.description: Expected position in flow execution order

columns.name: actual_order_nmbr
columns.description: Actual position if job executed

columns.name: job_stts_cd
columns.description: DM status code if job executed

columns.name: job_exctn_msg_txt
columns.description: Error message from DM (sanitized of PII)

columns.name: created_dttm
columns.description: When this exception was recorded

columns.name: notes
columns.description: Additional context or investigation notes

checkConstraints.name: CK_ValidationException_Type
checkConstraints.definition: ([exception_type]='UNKNOWN' OR [exception_type]='BUSINESS_REJECTION' OR [exception_type]='SYSTEM_FAILURE' OR [exception_type]='STUCK_JOB' OR [exception_type]='UNEXPECTED_JOB' OR [exception_type]='FAILED_JOB' OR [exception_type]='MISSING_JOB')

designNotes.summary: One row per discrepancy found during validation
designNotes.note: Each exception is a separate row, enabling flexible querying and aggregation across flows and exception types. The is_critical flag is set based on JobConfig.is_critical for the referenced job_id. The job_exctn_msg_txt field preserves DM error messages sanitized of PII.

statusValues.meaning: Job was expected from flow definition but did not execute.
Job executed but failed with zero records processed (true technical failure).
Job executed but was not in the expected job list from flow definition.

queries.name: Recent Exceptions
queries.description: All validation exceptions from the past 7 days with flow context from ValidationLog.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    vl.job_sqnc_shrt_nm AS flow_code,
    vl.execution_date,
    ve.exception_type,
    ve.job_shrt_nm,
    ve.is_critical,
    ve.job_exctn_msg_txt
FROM JobFlow.ValidationException ve
INNER JOIN JobFlow.ValidationLog vl ON vl.validation_id = ve.validation_id
WHERE ve.created_dttm >= DATEADD(DAY, -7, GETDATE())
ORDER BY ve.created_dttm DESC;

queries.name: Critical Exceptions Only
queries.description: Filtered view showing only exceptions on critical jobs — the ones that generate high-priority alerts.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    vl.job_sqnc_shrt_nm AS flow_code,
    vl.execution_date,
    ve.exception_type,
    ve.job_shrt_nm,
    ve.job_exctn_msg_txt
FROM JobFlow.ValidationException ve
INNER JOIN JobFlow.ValidationLog vl ON vl.validation_id = ve.validation_id
WHERE ve.is_critical = 1
ORDER BY ve.created_dttm DESC;

queries.name: Exception Counts by Type
queries.description: Aggregated exception counts over the past 30 days by type — shows which categories of issues are most common.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    exception_type,
    COUNT(*) AS exception_count,
    SUM(CAST(is_critical AS INT)) AS critical_count
FROM JobFlow.ValidationException
WHERE created_dttm >= DATEADD(DAY, -30, GETDATE())
GROUP BY exception_type
ORDER BY exception_count DESC;

queries.name: Frequently Failing Jobs
queries.description: Identifies jobs with repeated FAILED exceptions over the past 30 days — pattern detection for recurring issues.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    job_shrt_nm,
    COUNT(*) AS failure_count,
    MAX(created_dttm) AS last_failure
FROM JobFlow.ValidationException
WHERE exception_type = 'FAILED'
  AND created_dttm >= DATEADD(DAY, -30, GETDATE())
GROUP BY job_shrt_nm
ORDER BY failure_count DESC;

relationshipNotes.note: ValidationException.validation_id references ValidationLog.validation_id. Many exceptions per validation — one per discrepant job.

tables.name: ValidationLog
tables.description: Post-execution validation results capturing job count verification, missing job detection, and Jira ticket references for completed flow executions.

columns.name: validation_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for this validation record

columns.name: tracking_id
columns.description: Links to FlowExecutionTracking for the validated execution

columns.name: job_sqnc_id
columns.description: Flow ID for direct queries without join

columns.name: job_sqnc_shrt_nm
columns.description: Flow short name. Denormalized

columns.name: execution_date
columns.description: Date of the execution being validated

columns.name: validation_dttm
columns.description: When validation was performed

columns.name: validation_status
columns.description: Overall result: PASSED, FAILED, WARNING, PARTIAL

columns.name: expected_job_count
columns.description: Number of jobs expected from flow definition

columns.name: actual_job_count
columns.description: Number of jobs that actually executed

columns.name: missing_job_count
columns.description: Jobs expected but not executed

columns.name: unexpected_job_count
columns.description: Jobs executed but not in expected list

columns.name: failed_job_count
columns.description: Jobs that executed but failed

columns.name: critical_jobs_missing
columns.description: 1 if any missing jobs are marked is_critical in JobConfig

columns.name: critical_jobs_failed
columns.description: 1 if any failed jobs are marked is_critical in JobConfig

columns.name: missing_jobs_json
columns.description: JSON array of missing job details

columns.name: failed_jobs_json
columns.description: JSON array of failed job details with error messages

columns.name: jira_ticket_key
columns.description: Ticket key if validation failure generated a ticket (e.g., SD-12345)

columns.name: jira_ticket_url
columns.description: Full URL to the Jira ticket

columns.name: jira_ticket_created_dttm
columns.description: When the Jira ticket was created

columns.name: created_dttm
columns.description: When this validation record was created

checkConstraints.name: CK_ValidationLog_Status
checkConstraints.definition: ([validation_status]='FLOW_NOT_RUN' OR [validation_status]='BUSINESS_REJECTION' OR [validation_status]='PARTIAL_FAILURE' OR [validation_status]='CRITICAL_FAILURE' OR [validation_status]='SYSTEM_FAILURE' OR [validation_status]='MISSING_JOBS' OR [validation_status]='SUCCESS')

designNotes.summary: Runs after all jobs finish, not during execution
designNotes.note: Validation only runs after a flow reaches COMPLETE state (executing_job_count = 0, pending_job_count = 0). It compares expected_jobs_json from FlowExecutionTracking against actual job outcomes in JobExecutionLog. Missing, unexpected, and failed jobs are counted and classified. The flow then transitions to VALIDATED.

designNotes.summary: Validation failures can generate Jira tickets
designNotes.note: When validation detects critical failures (critical_jobs_missing = 1 or critical_jobs_failed = 1), a Jira ticket is created. The ticket key and URL are stored in jira_ticket_key and jira_ticket_url for cross-reference.

statusValues.meaning: All expected jobs executed successfully. No discrepancies found.
Expected jobs did not execute. May indicate cancelled flow or dependency failure.
Flow was detected but no jobs executed.
Some jobs had alertable failures but non-critical.
Critical jobs failed or were missing. Jira ticket generated.

queries.name: Recent Validation Results
queries.description: Validation outcomes for the past 7 days with job count comparisons and Jira ticket references.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    job_sqnc_shrt_nm AS flow_code,
    execution_date,
    validation_status,
    expected_job_count,
    actual_job_count,
    missing_job_count,
    failed_job_count,
    jira_ticket_key
FROM JobFlow.ValidationLog
WHERE validation_dttm >= DATEADD(DAY, -7, GETDATE())
ORDER BY validation_dttm DESC;

queries.name: Failed Validations
queries.description: Shows validations with FAILED or PARTIAL status including critical job flags and detail JSON for investigation.
queries.sql:
SELECT 
    job_sqnc_shrt_nm,
    execution_date,
    validation_status,
    critical_jobs_missing,
    critical_jobs_failed,
    missing_jobs_json,
    failed_jobs_json
FROM JobFlow.ValidationLog
WHERE validation_status IN ('FAILED', 'PARTIAL')
ORDER BY validation_dttm DESC;

relationshipNotes.note: ValidationLog.tracking_id references FlowExecutionTracking.tracking_id. One validation per completed execution.
ValidationException.validation_id references ValidationLog.validation_id. One summary row can have many exception detail rows.

scripts.name: Monitor-JobFlow.ps1
scripts.description: Core monitoring engine for Debt Manager job flows. Detects new flow executions, captures job completions, manages flow state transitions, detects system stalls, validates completed flows, and identifies missing scheduled flows. Runs every 5 minutes via the Orchestrator.
scripts.dataFlow: Reads: job_sqnc_log (flow executions), job_sqnc (flow definitions for ConfigSync), job_sqnc_exctn_log (flow-to-job linkage), job_log (job executions), job_entty_log (record-level results), job (job definitions), usr (user identification). All reads via AG secondary replica by default.

designNotes.summary: Two execution paths depending on job activity
designNotes.note: Step 0: ConfigSync — synchronize FlowConfig with DM. Step 1: DetectFlows — find new executions. Step 2: CaptureJobs — record completed jobs. Step 3: UpdateProgress — update flow job counts. Step 4: TransitionStates — manage state machine. Step 5: DetectStalls — snapshot comparison. Step 6: ValidateFlows — analyze completed flows. Step 7: DetectMissing — check for no-show flows. Each step updates its own row in JobFlow.Status. Not all steps run on every cycle — see the Early Exit design note.

designNotes.summary: Reads from configurable replica, writes to listener
designNotes.note: Automatically detects AG replica roles at startup via the listener. Uses GlobalConfig SourceReplica setting to select read server (PRIMARY or SECONDARY). Always writes to the xFACts listener. ForceSourceServer parameter can override for testing. If replica is unavailable, the script logs the failure and exits — retried on next orchestrator cycle.

designNotes.summary: Default execution is read-only
designNotes.note: Without the -Execute switch, the script runs in preview mode — reads all source data, evaluates all conditions, and displays what would happen without making any changes. Essential for testing and troubleshooting. The -Execute switch must be present for any writes to occur.

designNotes.topic: Early Exit on No Job Activity
designNotes.summary: Skips Steps 1-6 when DM has no active or pending jobs
designNotes.note: After ConfigSync, the script queries crs5_oltp.dbo.job_log for jobs in status 1 (Started) or 6 (Pending). If none are found, Steps 1 through 6 are skipped and only Step 7 (DetectMissing) runs before exit. This eliminates the heavy join against dbo.job_entty_log in Step 2 — a table with hundreds of millions of rows that generated significant read load on the DM secondary replica on every cycle regardless of whether any flows were executing. ConfigSync always runs because flow definition drift is independent of job activity. DetectMissing always runs because it specifically targets flows that have not started — gating it on job activity would prevent it from ever alerting.

relationshipNotes.note: Registered as module_name=JobFlow, process_name=Monitor-JobFlow, execution_mode=FIRE_AND_FORGET, interval_seconds=300. Reports completion via Complete-OrchestratorTask callback using TaskId and ProcessId parameters.

relationshipNotes.relatedObject: Reads: FlowConfig, JobConfig, Schedule, ErrorCategory, JobStatus
relationshipNotes.note: FlowConfig controls which flows are monitored and alert settings. JobConfig provides criticality flags for validation. Schedule defines expected execution windows. ErrorCategory classifies error patterns for alert decisions. JobStatus maps DM codes to xFACts statuses.
Reads dbo.GlobalConfig for: AGName (AG identification), SourceReplica (which replica to read from), StallThreshold (polls before stall alert, default 6).
